Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

It seems reasonable.  How does the caller know how long to wait before calling, or does it loop (with an interruptible timeout, maybe 0.1s) until one of the NIDs shows success?

Even then, this will only tell us which NIDs are alive (i.e. skip dead NIDs), but not which one is running the MGS.  We'll still need to loop over that, but hopefully will get -ENODEV back quickly.

Patch Set 1:

Mikhail, I believe PEER_DISCOVERED and PEER_NIDS_UPTODATE will be set in both "discovery on/off" cases if peer is discovered successfully. 
Even with discovery disabled, LNet is going to "discover" the peer. This can be verified by checking "peer state" in the "lnetctl peer show -v 4" output.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

I have another question about uptodate status in general - consider that remote node is restarted, so how long does it take for NID to change its status from updatodate to not-uptodate and back when node will return after some time? Is it enough to just have peer added once to LNET and all is done in background? Or re-discovery is started by some external events? I.e. what if NID is not uptodate, then we would never try it, but we must actually try it once to initiate discovery to get uptodate status, that is my point

Patch Set 3:

to the previous question - I am asking because I see that lnet_send() does discovery each time via lnet_select_pathway()->lnet_initiate_peer_discovery(). So it looks like if we will not try to send anything to peer then it will not be re-discovered

Patch Set 3:

If we take client's POV, unless there's communication with the server on the Lustre/LNet level (vs. LND only), the client may not find out that the server is gone as far as LNet layer is concerned (LND may find out, but won't necessarily tell LNet). So I think your concern is valid - LNet may think that a downed peer is "discovered and up-to-date". I'm hoping that change 53933 (when it is ready) takes care of this issue by forcing discovery on every NID of the peer in LNetAddPeer (and LNetPrimaryNID). LNetPeerDiscovered is expected to be called after LNetAddPeer/LNetPrimaryNID so it can report updated results.

Patch Set 3:

The problem with queuing discovery only in LNetPeerDiscovered() is that at that point the peer is already constructed via LNetAddPeer. This means that for sending discovery request, any of the listed peer NIDs may be selected. If the selected NID is down, we're wasting time - we have to wait for discovery to fail before issuing another one to the same peer. So it looks like we do have to use LNetAddPeer to send simultaneous discovery requests to every comma-separated NID, and to make it possible we need to make a separate peer for every such NID. Once discovery succeeds, peers should get merged back.

Patch Set 3: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne-arm on RHEL 8.8/aarch64, RHEL 8.8/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest 2 from onyx-91vm9: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/2bbe8e3d-233c-4a53-8bb6-ad988ab4b02e ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

> The problem with queuing discovery only in LNetPeerDiscovered()
I didn't mean 'only' there, I wonder more do we need to refresh discovery status time to time after peer added via LNetAddPeer()? And if not then why lnet_sent() does that always?

parents.subject: LU-17500 qmt: avoid "enforced bit set, but neither"

commit.subject: LU-17379 lnet: add LNetPeerDiscovered to LNet API
commit.message:
LU-17379 lnet: add LNetPeerDiscovered to LNet API

LNetPeerDiscovered is added to allow lustre check
whether the peer has been successfully discovered by LNet
before attempting to open a connection to it.
For example, given a mount command with a list of NIDs,
Lustre can use LNetAddPeer API to initiate discovery on
every candidate first, and later use LNetPeerDiscovered
to select a reachable peer to connect to.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I7c9964148a5a2a24d7889b8b4c2e488a433ca258
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53926
Reviewed-by: Frank Sehr <fsehr@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
